adopted_behavior: Barnes fill may remain flat or receive a machine-precision drainage gradient
Production accumulation uses Braun-Willett donor arrays and a drainage stack
Linear stream power uses the closed form; nonlinear n uses a bracketed Brent solve
Flooded and reversed-flow nodes are guarded against erosion
Explicit diffusion uses a conservative empirical stability prefactor of 0.15
known_deviations: The skill's fixed 1e-5 metre epsilon is an engine-scale policy, not Landlab's machine-precision default
The scalar reference diffusion oracle uses the textbook bound rather than Landlab's 0.15 production margin
entries.engine_translation: Use iterative owned stacks, fixed field buffers and explicit boundary flags; replace Python objects and SciPy roots with engine-native arrays and a bounded bracketed solver.

adopted_behavior: Priority-Flood plus epsilon uses nextafter rather than an arbitrary fixed increment
Perimeter cells establish the open/base-level boundary
D8 accumulation uses O(N) indegree/Kahn traversal
D-infinity snaps near-exact cardinal angles to one receiver within 1e-6 radians to prevent cycles
known_deviations: The skill exposes a world-unit epsilon because game vertical scales need an explicit reproducible policy
The readable numpy accumulator uses argsort and is not the production O(N) path
entries.engine_translation: Implement queue storage in persistent engine memory, encode nextafter or the declared world epsilon explicitly, and use iterative indegree traversal suitable for jobs or compute.

adopted_behavior: D8 uses a fixed N-NE-E-SE-S-SW-W-NW order with strict greater-than tie breaking
Flats and pits are explicit sentinel states
MFD weights downslope receivers by slope power and normalises the weights
The documented pipeline fills depressions and resolves flats before direction and accumulation
known_deviations: Tie order is implementation policy and differs from the skill's readable numpy neighbour order
The existing optional correlation test omits pysheds resolve_flats and is not a full pipeline equivalence test
entries.engine_translation: Choose and serialise one tie order, represent pit/flat states explicitly, and run fill-resolve-route-accumulate as separately versioned engine passes.

adopted_behavior: Sink resolution exposes priority-flood and Cordonnier-2019 MST carve/basic alternatives
Flow routing builds receiver/donor structures with bottom-up traversal indices
Linear stream power matches the Braun-Willett closed form
Nonlinear stream power uses guarded Newton iteration and single-flow routing
Stream-power updates clamp against flooded receiver elevation
Production geological diffusion may use unconditionally stable ADI
known_deviations: The skill's scalar stream-power oracle implements only the linear n=1 case
The skill's scalar diffusion oracle is explicit and CFL-limited rather than ADI
The MST depression algorithm is specifically Cordonnier et al. 2019, not the 2016 terrain application
entries.engine_translation: Keep the O(N) owned receiver stack, expose n=1 as the fast deterministic path, use a bounded nonlinear solve only when required, and map ADI to engine tridiagonal jobs or keep explicit diffusion for bounded runtime steps.

adopted_behavior: Axis-aligned 3D sampling uses an orthonormal lattice-rotated variant
Seed hashing is integer based and avoids a 256-entry permutation period
OpenSimplex2 uses a 128-entry 2D gradient table and its own normalisation
known_deviations: Generic Gustavson simplex pseudocode with an 8-gradient set and scale 70 is not OpenSimplex2 proper
OpenSimplex2 is F-tier source-grounded practice rather than a peer-reviewed algorithm paper
entries.engine_translation: Use engine-native integer hashing and constants from the neutral specification, expose rotated 3D variants explicitly, and verify continuity, range and directional spectra.

adopted_behavior: Noise, fractal and domain-warp policies compose instead of duplicating each algorithm
Cellular distance and return modes are explicit first-class choices
Two-dimensional and three-dimensional OpenSimplex2-labelled paths use different algorithms and constants
known_deviations: FastNoiseLite's 2D OpenSimplex2 dispatch is classic simplex rather than KdotJPG's A2-star implementation
Empirical output normalisers are implementation-specific and must not be mixed between variants
entries.engine_translation: Preserve the compositional API shape, generate CPU/GPU variants from one constant set, and version the exact noise variant because equal UI names do not imply equal output.

adopted_behavior: Droplet direction blends inertia with the local gradient
Capacity depends on downhill delta, speed and water
Erosion uses a radius brush while deposition is bilinear and point-local
A reusable translation-invariant brush can be precomputed and uploaded
known_deviations: CPU and GPU paths use different boundary stopping policies
The GPU path performs overlapping droplet writes without atomics and is not deterministic
This is a popular MIT implementation after Beyer rather than a canonical peer-reviewed droplet paper
entries.engine_translation: Use the brush and capacity behavior but redesign GPU writes with batching, deterministic accumulation or explicitly documented nondeterminism; keep CPU/GPU boundary policy identical.

adopted_behavior: Virtual-pipe flux is scaled so one step cannot export more water than a cell owns
The sediment-capacity path includes the Stava shallow-water lmax ramp
The GPU pipeline is staged across flux, velocity, erosion/deposition and transport passes
known_deviations: The implementation uses four pipes rather than the skill's eight-direction production recommendation
Its thermal pass is four-neighbour and self-documents an incorrect uniform cell-size assumption for non-square cells
The hardness-softening extension is implementation-specific
entries.engine_translation: Keep staged GPU resources and the outflow scale, upgrade to distance-correct eight-direction stencils where radial quality matters, and use the skill's stricter thermal oracle instead of inheriting the known cell-size defect.

adopted_behavior: Acceleration grid cell size is 0.999 times radius divided by sqrt(dimensions)
Default candidate attempts are 30
Active-list removal is O(1) swap-with-last
Candidates are generated in the annulus from r to 2r with deterministic seeded sampling
known_deviations: The 0.999 numerical margin is an implementation hardening beyond the paper's ideal r/sqrt(n)
Runtime hierarchical scatter still requires world-region ownership and stable chunk boundary policy
entries.engine_translation: Use fixed allocator or GPU buffers, stable region-seeded active lists and shared-border ownership; verify minimum distance and blue-noise spectra.
